The move appears partially "transient" in that the magnitude exceeds the guidance revision alone. The stock's sensitivity to nutrition weakness (a ~16% segment of sales) being amplified by -10% suggests either (1) market repricing earnings power if nutrition recovery stalls, (2) concern about management's other guidance (e.g., margin expansion, Exact Sciences integration), or (3) sector rotation / macro risk aversion on the day. The 10% drop on a 50-bps guidance cut and a nutrition segment that is being strategically reset argues for a "mixed" or "transient" characterization; I lean toward "structural" because the magnitude of the nutrition decline itself (down 9% in Q4) represents a real earnings headwind that will persist until 2H 2026 recovery, and tariff/China macro risks remain.
